header.title: Hreflang Tag Generator — Multilingual SEO Made Simple
header.description: Free online hreflang tag generator for multilingual and multi-regional websites. Generate HTML link tags, XML sitemap hreflang annotations, and HTTP headers. Supports 70+ languages and 60+ regions with x-default fallback. Essential for international SEO and avoiding duplicate content across language versions.
header.keywords: hreflang generator, hreflang tag generator, multilingual seo, hreflang tags, international seo, language tags, multi-regional seo

whatIsHreflang.title: What is hreflang?
whatIsHreflang.description: Hreflang tells search engines which language and region a page targets, and which alternate versions exist for other locales.
uses: Same language, different regions (en-US vs en-GB)
Alternate versions with different regional content
whatIsHreflang.xDefault: x-default = the fallback page shown when no other hreflang matches the user's language.

rules.title: Must-follow Rules
items: Every page must reference ALL other language versions
Every page must also reference itself
All URLs must be absolute (with https://)
Tags must be in the <head> of every page
Or use XML sitemap / HTTP headers instead
Hreflang is confirmed — Google still decides which to show

implementation.description: These tags must be copied to the {head} of every page in the set — including all language versions. Each page must link to all others, including itself.

xmlHint.description: In the XML sitemap, each {url} block must contain all {xhtmlLink} alternates. Submit this sitemap in Google Search Console.

howToUse.title: How to Use Hreflang Tag Generator
howToUse.step1Desc: Paste the URL of the page you want to create hreflang tags for
howToUse.step2Desc: Add all language and regional versions of the page with their URLs
howToUse.step3Desc: Choose between HTML link tags, XML sitemap annotations, or HTTP headers
howToUse.step4Desc: Copy the generated tags and add them to the <head> of every page in the set

examples.title: Try an Example
examples.stats: 3 language versions (en, es, fr) · x-default configured

items.question: What is hreflang used for?
items.answer: Hreflang tags tell search engines which language or regional version of a page to serve to users based on their language and location preferences.

items.question: Do I need hreflang for same-language different countries?
items.answer: Yes, if you have similar content targeting different regions (e.g., US English vs UK English), hreflang tags help serve the correct regional version.

items.question: Can I use hreflang without a self-reference?
items.answer: No, each hreflang annotation must include a self-referencing canonical link. Every URL in the group must reference itself as a canonical.

items.question: What is the x-default hreflang tag?
items.answer: The x-default hreflang tag specifies a fallback page for users whose language or region does not match any specific hreflang entry.
